subject: Human Resources
title: Pay for Performance Debates
description: A micro-lesson that makes Pay for Performance Debates usable.

hook.text: A company gives everyone the same 3% raise. Top performers are furious. Low performers are relieved. Is this fair?

buildup.text: Pay for performance links compensation increases, bonuses, or equity to individual performance ratings and outcomes.

discovery.text: Proponents say it motivates effort and rewards contribution. Critics say it creates competition, gaming, and rating inflation.

twist.text: When the gap between a '3' and '4' rating is $500, the incentive won't change behavior—but the resentment is real.

climax.text: The most effective approaches differentiate meaningfully—significant rewards for top performance, honest talks for everyone else.

punchline.text: If you're going to link pay to performance, make the link worth fighting for.

quiz.question: What is a common criticism of pay-for-performance systems?
options: They create competition, gaming, and rating inflation
